summary: Added the reusable Keyword Expansion Agent with typed Zod input/output schemas, docs-aligned prompt construction, safeJsonCompletion wiring through an injected LlmClient, deterministic mock fallback, focused tests, and README documentation.

tests_run.result: Passed: 16 tests across 5 suites, including Keyword Expansion Agent LLM success, malformed-output fallback, excluded-topic filtering, deterministic fallback output, requested count limits, existing LLM JSON utility tests, and existing mock SERP provider tests.
Passed: Next.js production build completed. Build emitted @next/swc-win32-x64-msvc invalid Win32 application warnings but compiled, type-checked, generated static pages, and exited successfully.

decisions: Kept the agent side-effect free: no process.env reads, network calls, database writes, provider configuration, API route, UI, or scan orchestration were added.
Used the existing services/llm public API only; safeJsonCompletion is called only when an LlmClient is injected.
Defaulted to 20 candidates and capped requested counts at 50 while preserving zero-count requests.
Normalized keyword, country, language, rationale, intent, and tool fields before returning candidates.
Applied a case-insensitive excludedTopics guard to candidate keyword and rationale text, then supplemented short LLM results with deterministic mock candidates when needed.
risks: The deterministic fallback is intentionally template-based; later scan-quality work may tune templates after SERP and scoring feedback exists.
The agent returns in-memory candidates only. Persistence remains deferred to later scan orchestrator/API work as required by this scoped task.
Next build still reports the existing SWC Windows native package warning despite successful exit.
requested_action: Review the new agents/ boundary, prompt/schema alignment, fallback behavior, excluded-topic guard, and no-secret/no-network/no-persistence policy for acceptance.
